summary: Crystal‑clear 1968 soundboard capture of a jazzy, psychedelic set highlighted by a scorching “Clementine” and an unforgettable (though truncated) Dark Star.
review: The January 20 1968 Eureka show is praised as one of the band’s most vibrant early performances – a tight, swing‑infused jam that showcases a rare Lesh/Hunter composition (“Clementine”), a fiery Pigpen organ burst and a blistering Dark Star. The Miller soundboard source is described as crisp, clean and “the best sounding file archived”, with only minor complaints about missing portions of the set.

ai_rating.rationale: Over two dozen reviews (most 5‑star) consistently rave about both the musical brilliance and the pristine soundboard recording; only a few minor criticisms about cuts keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean, well‑balanced mix praised as the best sounding archive file; occasional early fade‑out of Dark Star and missing intro/outro segments noted.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Shorter than a typical ’68 show but flows like one continuous, jazzy suite; reviewers describe it as “one long song” with superb swing feel.

band_member_comments.Jerry: General praise for the band’s groove and psychedelic vibe; guitar work noted as “mastery of the groove”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted as “on fire”, with several reviewers calling out his strong playing.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ praised as “very good”; keyboards noted for jazzy swing (cymbal‑focused rhythm) and a “My Favorite Things” vibe on Clementine.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming described as swinging, with emphatic cymbal work that drives the jazz‑inflected feel.
